name: Stheno
description: Stheno, meaning 'forceful,' was one of the three Gorgon sisters in Greek mythology, along with Medusa and Euryale. Unlike Medusa, Stheno and Euryale were immortal. She was renowned for her ferocity and strength, embodying the terrifying aspects of the Gorgons. Stheno, like her sisters, possessed venomous snakes for hair and a gaze that could turn onlookers to stone. While Medusa is more widely known due to her encounter with Perseus, Stheno was a formidable monster in her own right, a symbol of primal fear and the dangers of the natural world. Her existence served as a warning against hubris and the potential consequences of challenging the gods or entering forbidden territories. Stheno's role in the Gorgon mythos highlights the ancient Greeks' fascination with monstrous beings and their representation of chaos, danger, and the untamed aspects of nature. She represents a powerful and dangerous entity, a creature to be feared and avoided at all costs. While details of her individual exploits are scarce compared to Medusa, her presence as an immortal Gorgon underscores the pervasive threat posed by these mythical beings. The Gorgons, collectively, represent the fearsome power of female figures in Greek mythology, capable of inflicting destruction and inspiring terror.
subtitle: Forceful Gorgon of Greek Myth
appearance: Stheno is depicted as a monstrous woman with bronze hands, golden wings, and venomous snakes for hair. Her face is often described as grotesque and terrifying, capable of instilling immediate fear. Her eyes possess the power to turn anyone who gazes upon them directly into stone. She has sharp claws and a powerful physique, befitting her role as a fearsome Gorgon.
habitat: The Gorgons were said to inhabit remote and desolate locations, often near the edge of the known world. Their lair was a place of darkness and despair, filled with the petrified remains of those who had dared to trespass.
behavior: Stheno is characterized by her fierce and aggressive nature. Driven by a primal instinct to protect herself and her sisters, she is quick to attack any intruders. She is unrelenting in her pursuit of prey and shows no mercy to those who cross her path. Her immortal status fuels her sense of superiority and makes her an even more formidable foe.

keyMyths.title: The Gorgons' Origins
keyMyths.description: The Gorgons were originally beautiful women, but were cursed by Athena, transforming them into monstrous creatures with snakes for hair. This curse was a punishment for either Medusa's vanity or for being defiled by Poseidon in Athena's temple.

keyMyths.title: Perseus and Medusa
keyMyths.description: While Stheno wasn't directly involved in Perseus's quest, the myth of Perseus slaying Medusa illustrates the danger and power of the Gorgons. The other Gorgons sought to avenge Medusa, but Perseus escaped using Hermes' winged sandals and Hades' helm of invisibility.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive account of Greek mythology, including the Gorgons' lineage and connection to Perseus.
sources.text: A comprehensive account of Greek mythology, including the Gorgons' lineage and connection to Perseus.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Provides an early account of the Gorgons, listing their parentage and describing them as fearsome monsters.
sources.text: Provides an early account of the Gorgons, listing their parentage and describing them as fearsome monsters.

sources.author: Hyginus
sources.description: Offers alternative versions of the Gorgons' origins and their relationship to the gods.
